title: One Piece You've Been Told Is Too Young
subtitle: A three-minute activity for wearing the thing someone has said you cannot
excerpt: A three-minute activity for the woman who has been told, often gently, that something she likes is too young for her now. The shoes, the dress, the lipstick, the colour of the hair. The activity is to wear the thing, in the world, for one full day this week.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The wear-the-thing-anyway practice is a staple of writing on midlife style and on self-trust.

practice.whenToUse: Use when there is one item in your wardrobe that you love and have stopped wearing because of a comment, a sideways look, or a quiet inner rule about what is age-appropriate.

content.text: A three-minute activity for the item of clothing you have stopped wearing because someone told you it was too young. The activity is to put it on this week and wear it in the world for one full day.
New to activity as a practice? Read

Most of us have one. A dress, a top, a shoe, a lipstick, a colour. We loved it. Someone said something. Maybe it was a friend who meant well. Maybe it was a comment in a magazine. Maybe it was just the look on a face.
The item went to the back of the wardrobe. The colour faded out of the rotation. The lipstick stayed in the drawer.
Self-trust in midlife is built one move at a time. Wearing the thing you have been told you should not wear is one of the cheapest moves: no money, no time, no big plan.
The day you wear it, you find out what the world actually says about it. Most of the time, the world says nothing. The voice that told you not to was in your head.
Pick the item this week. One item, not three.
Pick the day. A day where you will be out in the world for at least part of it.
Put it on in the morning. Wear it the whole day, even if the second-guess voice gets loud.
In the evening, write one sentence in your phone or a notebook: what was the day like in the thing.
Original to homemade.education. The wear-it-anyway move is a common one. It shows up in many books on midlife and style.
